pattern: Tighten each restriction by sweeping the slope limits forward and backward, then find the tallest peak each gap between adjacent restrictions can support.
hints: Building 1 is fixed at height 0 and neighbors differ by at most one, so each restriction also caps its neighbors transitively.
Sort restrictions by position and relax each cap using its neighbors on the left, then on the right, to get the true achievable heights.
Between two adjacent fixed buildings the height can rise then fall in a tent; the peak is (gap + leftHeight + rightHeight) // 2, and also check the open end after the last restriction.

edge_inputs.description: two restrictions force a low profile
restrictions out of order with a tall interior peak
a single restriction at the far end
a high cap is limited by the slope from building 1
two restrictions where the tent peaks between them

stress.description: small n with random restrictions so a direct simulation oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 600))
    cnt = rng.randrange(0, min(n - 1, 40) + 1)
    if cnt == 0:
        return [n, []]
    ids = rng.sample(range(2, n + 1), cnt)
    res = [[i, rng.randrange(0, n + 5)] for i in ids]
    return [n, res]
stress.note: n bounded so the O(n) simulation oracle is cheap; the optimal handles huge n
